steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure of the text. Map out the three moves the passage makes before matching to the choices.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'defines an approach, then adds public opinion commentary': The passage lists concrete benefits, not public opinion. ✗
  • Choice B — 'treats a study as the main subject': The Grace Herzel butterfly study is an example at the end, not the primary focus. ✗
  • Choice C — 'includes a development narrative and future suggestions': No development history or future suggestions appear in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'identifies a research approach, lists its benefits, then mentions a study using that approach': Matches all three moves: community science is defined and called effective, benefits are listed, and the Arkansas butterfly study is cited as an example. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
